name: Anna O'Brien's
tagline: Honolulu's quintessential dive bar since 1969 — live rock, reggae, and punk upstairs, sticky-floor charm downstairs.
talkStory: Named after original owner Anna O'Brien who opened this Mo'ili'ili dive in 1969, it's outlasted every trendy bar in Honolulu. The upstairs stage launched careers for local punk and reggae acts, and the no-cover policy most nights keeps it democratic. Anthony Bourdain drank here and called it his kind of place. The motorcycle club regulars and UH rugby teams treat it like a second living room.
